cta.title: Not Sure Whether Our Care Is Right for You?
cta.description: Contact us to arrange a complimentary consultation and discuss your individual health concerns with our team.

hero.title: Conditions We Help Support
hero.subtitle: Whether you're dealing with everyday aches or more complex chronic issues, Traditional Chinese Medicine offers natural, evidence-informed care for a wide range of health concerns.

categories.name: Pain Management
categories.subtitle: Compassionate, medication-free support for recent injuries and ongoing pain conditions
categories.description: Acupuncture and TCM therapies can help address lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica, whether symptoms appeared suddenly or have continued for a long time, without depending on medications. - In TCM, neck and shoulder pain is often related to Qi stagnation and blood stasis. - Acupuncture may be used to release trigger points and reduce localized tightness. - Tui Na helps loosen tense muscles, and cupping encourages better circulation in the affected area.

categories.name: Emotional & Mental Wellbeing
categories.description: Supportive care for stress, anxiety, depression, and emotional wellbeing overall

categories.name: Digestive Health
categories.description: Focused support for digestive conditions and lasting gut health

categories.name: Women's Health
categories.description: Whole-person, attentive care for a broad range of women's health concerns

categories.name: Respiratory Issues
categories.description: Gentle care to support respiratory health and comfortable breathing

categories.name: Immune System
categories.description: Support your body's natural defenses and help strengthen overall resilience

conditions.title: Lower Back, Upper Back & Sciatica Pain
conditions.description: Acupuncture and TCM therapies provide natural support for lower back pain, upper back pain, and sciatica, helping with both recent flare-ups and long-term symptoms without drugs or surgery.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, back pain is viewed as impaired flow of Qi and blood through the body's channels. Our practitioners use acupuncture to help clear these blockages, cupping to encourage healthy circulation, and herbal medicine to support inflammation management and strengthen the back.

conditions.title: Pain in the Neck & Shoulders
conditions.description: Neck and shoulder tension, stiffness, and aching can develop from stress, posture, or injury, and may respond well to targeted natural therapies.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, neck and shoulder pain is commonly associated with Qi stagnation and blood stasis affecting the channels in that area. We use acupuncture to release trigger points, Tui Na to reduce muscle tightness, and cupping to improve circulation.

conditions.title: Joint Pain & Arthritis
symptoms: Pain that worsens when the weather changes
conditions.description: Natural care to help manage pain related to osteoarthritis, rheumatoid arthritis, and general inflammation in the joints.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, arthritis falls under 'Bi Syndrome,' where wind, cold, dampness, or heat obstructs the joints. Treatment focuses on clearing these pathogenic factors, easing inflammation, and supporting the body's overall strength and resilience.

conditions.title: Migraines & Headaches
conditions.description: We offer natural, medication-free support for tension headaches, migraines, and ongoing head pain.
conditions.tcmApproach: TCM identifies several headache patterns, including Liver Yang rising, Blood deficiency, and external pathogenic influences. Once we determine the pattern that fits your presentation, we create a treatment plan with acupuncture and herbal medicine to address the underlying imbalance.

conditions.title: Anxiety & Panic Disorder
conditions.description: Natural, medication-free support for people experiencing anxiety, panic attacks, and ongoing nervousness.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, anxiety is often connected to Heart and Kidney disharmony, Liver Qi stagnation, or disturbance of the Shen (spirit). Treatment is aimed at calming the Shen, restoring the smooth movement of Qi, and nourishing the underlying organ systems to support emotional balance.

conditions.title: Depression & Persistent Low Mood
symptoms: Difficulty with focus and clear thinking
conditions.description: Whole-person care for those dealing with depression, low energy, and reduced motivation.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, depression is often linked to Liver Qi stagnation, Heart Blood deficiency, or Spleen Qi deficiency. Treatment focuses on moving stagnant Qi, addressing underlying deficiencies, and gently supporting improved mood and vitality.

conditions.title: Insomnia & Sleep Problems
conditions.description: Natural, supportive care for people who have trouble falling asleep, wake often during the night, or regularly feel they are not getting restful sleep.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, sleep disruption is often linked to imbalance between the Heart and Kidney, Yin deficiency creating internal heat, or agitation of the Shen. Treatment is directed toward nourishing Yin, calming the Heart, and settling the Shen to support deeper, more restorative sleep.

conditions.title: Stress and Burnout
conditions.description: Restore steadiness and resilience when ongoing stress or burnout has affected your overall wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: Chronic stress can gradually deplete Qi and contribute to Liver Qi stagnation throughout the body. Our approach focuses on smoothing and regulating Liver Qi, addressing underlying deficiencies, and incorporating practical stress management tools to help rebuild balance and support long-term resilience.

conditions.title: IBS and Digestive Issues
conditions.description: Focused support for irritable bowel syndrome, bloating, gas, and common forms of digestive discomfort.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, IBS often involves a mismatch between the Liver and Spleen, with emotional stress disrupting digestion. Care is aimed at moving Liver Qi, strengthening Spleen function, and restoring better digestive balance.

conditions.title: Acid Reflux and GERD
conditions.description: Natural TCM-based supportive care for heartburn, acid reflux, and gastroesophageal reflux disease.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, reflux is seen as Stomach Qi rising upward instead of descending properly, often with heat or phlegm present. Treatment focuses on directing Stomach Qi downward, clearing related heat, and strengthening digestion overall.

conditions.title: Chronic Constipation
symptoms: Bowel movements that are infrequent or irregular
conditions.description: Personalized, supportive care for chronic constipation and irregular bowel habits.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, constipation may stem from different underlying patterns, such as Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, Yin deficiency, or weakness of Spleen Qi. Because each person's root pattern may differ, treatment is individualized with the aim of supporting more regular, healthy bowel function.

conditions.title: Fertility and Conception Support
conditions.description: Whole-person care for individuals pursuing natural conception or preparing for IVF, including support for both women and men.
conditions.tcmApproach: Our approach focuses on regulating the menstrual cycle, nourishing Kidney Essence (Jing), promoting healthy circulation to the reproductive organs, and reducing stress. Treatment plans are tailored to each partner's individual needs and TCM patterns.

conditions.title: Menstrual Concerns
conditions.description: Natural supportive care for painful periods, irregular cycles, PMS, and other menstrual-related concerns.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, menstrual concerns often relate to Liver Qi stagnation, Blood deficiency, or Cold in the uterus. Treatment works to regulate the cycle, nourish and move Blood, support the smooth flow of Qi, and address pathogenic factors that may be contributing to symptoms.

conditions.title: Menopause-Related Symptoms
conditions.description: Warm, natural support for hot flashes, night sweats, mood changes, and other symptoms that can come with the menopausal transition.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, menopause is understood as a natural decline in Kidney Yin and Kidney Yang. Care is aimed at nourishing Kidney Yin, improving harmony between Yin and Yang, and calming the Shen to help ease the transition and support symptom relief.

conditions.title: PCOS and Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
symptoms: Irregular menstrual cycles or no menstruation
conditions.description: A whole-person approach for supporting hormonal balance, encouraging more regular cycles, and helping manage PCOS symptoms.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, PCOS commonly involves a combination of Kidney deficiency, Phlegm-Damp accumulation, and Liver Qi stagnation. Treatment emphasizes transforming phlegm, regulating and moving Qi, and strengthening Kidney function to support overall balance.

conditions.title: Seasonal Allergies and Hay Fever
conditions.description: Supportive natural care for seasonal allergies, hay fever, and sensitivity to environmental triggers.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, allergic reactions often suggest weakness in Wei Qi, the body's defensive Qi, along with other internal imbalances. Our approach works to strengthen Wei Qi, clear pathogenic factors, and help settle inflammatory responses.

conditions.title: Asthma Support
symptoms: Symptoms brought on by physical activity
conditions.description: Complementary TCM care to support asthma management and help reduce the frequency and intensity of episodes.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM perspective, asthma is often associated with weakness in the Lung and Kidney together with phlegm in the airways. Treatment focuses on strengthening Lung and Kidney function, resolving phlegm, and helping reduce overall sensitivity.

conditions.title: Persistent Chronic Cough
conditions.description: Supportive care for an ongoing cough that continues even after conventional treatment has been tried.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, a chronic cough may arise from different pattern presentations, including Lung Yin deficiency, Lung Heat, or Phlegm buildup. Once the underlying pattern is identified, treatment is tailored to the specific imbalance involved.

conditions.title: Recurring Colds & Flu
conditions.description: We focus on supporting immune function to help reduce how frequently you become ill and lessen the effects of colds and respiratory infections when they happen.
conditions.tcmApproach: In TCM, getting sick repeatedly is often associated with Wei Qi deficiency. Treatment centers on strengthening Wei Qi, supporting the Lung and Spleen, and gradually improving the body's overall immune resilience.

conditions.title: Persistent Fatigue
symptoms: Finding it hard to manage everyday activities
conditions.description: If ongoing exhaustion and low energy are affecting daily life, our care is aimed at supporting the body's natural vitality and helping you regain a greater sense of wellbeing.
conditions.tcmApproach: From a TCM standpoint, persistent fatigue commonly relates to Qi deficiency involving the Spleen, the Kidneys, or both, and it may also include Blood deficiency. Treatment focuses on addressing these underlying deficiencies to support the body's energy and vitality.
treatmentMethods: Guidance for balancing rest with physical activity

introduction.note: Don’t see your condition listed here? Please contact us — we may still be able to support your care.
introduction.text: At Gang Shi Acupuncture, we focus on caring for the whole person rather than only treating symptoms. Our approach looks at both the root causes of illness and the discomfort you may be feeling right now. The conditions below are some of the concerns we commonly support in our practice.
